summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser have addressed the handling of HLS (HTTP Live Streaming), a technology that allows streaming media over the internet. The changes involve disabling the HLS player when running in an isolated process, which is a security feature designed to protect user data by separating web content from the main browsing process. This adjustment matters because it enhances the security of streaming experiences, ensuring that sensitive information remains safeguarded while users enjoy their content. **Users can feel more secure when streaming, knowing that potential risks from isolated processes are being actively managed.**
